title: OSP Profile: Legal Advisory v1
description: Vocabulary for legal services: advisory, transactional, and contentious work. Captures the parameters an agent needs to decide whether a provider can actually take on a matter in a given jurisdiction and practice area, as opposed to generic professional-services criteria.

items.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, optionally with subdivision. Examples: 'CH', 'CH-ZH', 'US-NY', 'DE', 'EU'.

jurisdictions.description: Jurisdictions in which the provider is qualified to advise or act. A missing jurisdiction is a hard exclusion, not a soft preference.

practice_areas.description: Legal practice areas the provider covers. Agents should compare on this list before any other field.

matter_types.description: Modes of engagement the provider handles.

bar_admissions.description: Bar associations, law societies, or equivalent registrations under which the firm or its lawyers practise. Free text to accommodate non-ISO naming. Example: 'Zürcher Anwaltsverband', 'NY State Bar'.

court_representation.description: Levels of court or tribunal at which the provider can appear. Important distinction because many advisory firms do not litigate.

languages_of_work.description: Languages in which the provider drafts, advises, and conducts proceedings. Not the same as general website languages.

billing_models.description: Available billing arrangements. Some jurisdictions prohibit contingency — declaring it signals availability, not legality in every listed jurisdiction.

privilege_regime.description: Privilege framework the provider operates under. Matters for cross-border and in-house contexts where privilege recognition varies.

conflict_check_process.description: How conflicts of interest are screened before engagement. Agents acting for corporate clients should require this.

ethical_walls_supported.description: Whether the provider operates information barriers to allow simultaneous engagements that would otherwise conflict.

regulated_industry_experience.description: Regulated sectors in which the provider has declared meaningful experience. Not a certification; a self-declaration used for matching.

team_composition.description: Indicative staffing capacity. Lets an agent distinguish a solo practitioner from a full-service firm.

cross_border_capability.description: Whether the provider routinely handles matters spanning multiple jurisdictions in-house, as opposed to referring abroad.
